zh.lifeSummary: 當Diepold Graf von Lechsgemünd於大約1140年出生在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 斯塔恩格安德斯時，他的父親Volkrat von Lechsgemünd Frontenhausen22歲，母親Luitgard von Vohburg21歲。他於大約1170年與Agatha von Teck結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1192年7月1日享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Diepold Graf von Lechsgemünd于大约1140年出生在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 斯塔恩格安德斯时，他的父亲Volkrat von Lechsgemünd Frontenhausen22岁，母亲Luitgard von Vohburg21岁。他于大约1170年与Agatha von Teck结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1192年7月1日享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: Graf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Grebe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Fritz, Hans, Willi.
German (Hesse): variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: De Graaf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Adriaan, Bastiaan, Cornelis, Dirk, Geert.

Dutch: from graaf ‘count’ (see Graf ), with the addition of the definite article de ‘the’, hence a nickname for the servant of a count or for someone supposedly resembling one, and also a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named with this word.

variants.name: Graefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Arno, Goetz, Horst, Johannes.
German (North Rhine-Westphalia; also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Grefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhardt, Gerhard, Gerhart, Kurt.
North German: variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: Greve
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Otto, Reinhold, Bernhardt, Claus, Erna, Erwin, Ewald, Fritz, Kurt.
North German and Scandinavian: status name from Middle Low German and Danish greve ‘count’, equivalent to German Graf .
Dutch: vowel variant of Grave ‘count’, or a topographic name from Middle Dutch greve ‘ditch, moat, channel’.

variants.name: Croff
meanings: Altered form of German   Graf   or   Graff  . Or of Dutch   Kroft  .

variants.name: Mayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Franz, Helmut, Wilhelm, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Lorenz, Wolfgang.

English (Staffordshire): post-medieval form of Mear, which is either a topographic name, from Middle English mere ‘pond, pool’ (Old English mere) or Middle English mere ‘boundary’ (Old English (ge)mǣre), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places with names derived from these terms, such as Mere (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire), Meir in Caverswall (Staffordshire), and Meare (Somerset).
English: occupational name for a physician, from Old French mire, Middle English mire, meir, meyre, originally synonymous with Myer .

variants.name: Grafe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ralf.
German (also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Graef
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
German (also Gräf): see Graf .
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graff
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Diepold
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: from a variant of the personal name Dietbald (see Theobald ).

variants.name: Theobald
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from þeud ‘people, race’ + bald ‘bold, brave’. The first element was altered under the influence of Greek theos ‘god’. This name was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain.

en.lifeSummary: When Diepold Graf von Lechsgemünd was born about 1140, in Andechs, Starnberg, Bavaria, Germany, his father, Volkrat von Lechsgemünd Frontenhausen, was 22 and his mother, Luitgard von Vohburg, was 21. He married Agatha von Teck about 1170. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 1 July 1192, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Diepold Graf von Lechsgemünd est né environ 1140, à Andechs, Landkreis Starnberg, Bayern, Allemagne, son père, Volkrat von Lechsgemünd Frontenhausen, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Luitgard von Vohburg, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Agatha von Teck environ 1170. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé le 1 juillet 1192, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Diepold Graf von Lechsgemünd um 1140 in Andechs, Landkreis Starnberg, Bayern, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Volkrat von Lechsgemünd Frontenhausen 22 und seine Mutter Luitgard von Vohburg 21. Er hat um 1170 Agatha von Teck geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb am 1. Juli 1192 im Alter von 53 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Diepold Graf von Lechsgemünd è nato il circa 1140 nel Andechs, Landkreis Starnberg, Bayern, Germania, il suo padre, Volkrat von Lechsgemünd Frontenhausen, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Luitgard von Vohburg, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Agatha von Teck circa 1170. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 1 luglio 1192, all'età di 53 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Diepold Graf von Lechsgemündが約1140年にドイツバイエルンランドクレイス スターンバーグアンデチスで生まれた時，父Volkrat von Lechsgemünd Frontenhausenは22歳，母Luitgard von Vohburgは21歳であった。約1170年にAgatha von Teckと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1192年7月1日に53歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Diepold Graf von Lechsgemünd(이)가 대략 1140년 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 스타른버그 앤데크스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Volkrat von Lechsgemünd Frontenhausen(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Luitgard von Vohburg(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1170년 Agatha von Teck(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1192년 7월 1일 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Diepold Graf von Lechsgemünd nasceu aproximadamente 1140, em Andechs, Landkreis Starnberg, Bayern, Alemanha, seu pai, Volkrat von Lechsgemünd Frontenhausen, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Luitgard von Vohburg, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Agatha von Teck aproximadamente 1170. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1 de julho de 1192, com 53 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Diepold Graf von Lechsgemünd родился около 1140 г. в местности андечс, ландкрис старнбирг, беиирн, Германия, его отцу по имени Volkrat von Lechsgemünd Frontenhausen было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Luitgard von Vohburg, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Agatha von Teck около 1170 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 1 июля 1192 г. в возрасте 53 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Diepold Graf von Lechsgemünd nació aproximadamente 1140, en Andechs, Landkreis Starnberg, Bayern, Alemania, su papá, Volkrat von Lechsgemünd Frontenhausen, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Luitgard von Vohburg, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Agatha von Teck aproximadamente 1170. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 1 de julio de 1192, a la edad de 53 años.
